<!-- Topic 7: Programming Task — Parallel Dynamic Arrays -->
<!-- Total slides: 3 -->

# Programming Task — Parallel Dynamic Arrays

---

## Build it: student names and scores on the heap {.smaller}

+ Allocate two parallel dynamic arrays — one for names (`string*`), one for scores (`int*`).
+ Read n entries from the user, find the highest score, print that student's name, then release both arrays.

::: notes
Total slides: 3
Give students ~10 minutes to write this from scratch. They have seen all the pieces — new[], delete[], parallel array indexing. The challenge is managing two heap allocations together and releasing both cleanly.
:::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

---

## Requirements

- Ask the user for the number of students `n`
- Allocate `string* names = new string[n]` and `int* scores = new int[n]`
- Read each student's name and score from the user
- Find the index of the highest score
- Print: `"Top student: [name] with [score]"`
- Release both arrays with `delete[]` and set both pointers to `nullptr`

::: notes
Remind students: two allocations means two delete[] calls. Forgetting one is a leak. This task is designed to make that concrete.
:::

<!-- Slide 2 -->

---

## Solution {.smaller}

```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
using namespace std;

int main() {
    int n;
    cout << "Number of students: ";
    cin >> n;

    string* names  = new string[n];
    int*    scores = new int[n];

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        cout << "Name:  "; cin >> names[i];
        cout << "Score: "; cin >> scores[i];
    }

    int best = 0;
    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++)
        if (scores[i] > scores[best]) best = i;

    cout << "Top student: " << names[best]
         << " with " << scores[best] << "\n";

    delete[] names;   names  = nullptr;
    delete[] scores;  scores = nullptr;

    return 0;
}
```

<!-- Slide 3 -->
